args.code:
import pandas as pd

business_data = pd.DataFrame(locals()['var_function-call-6000310372648994279'])
review_data = pd.DataFrame(locals()['var_function-call-4022321191204377291'])

# Convert 'rating' column to numeric, handling potential errors by coercing to NaN
review_data['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(review_data['rating'], errors='coerce')

# Drop rows where rating is NaN (if any)
review_data.dropna(subset=['rating'], inplace=True)

# Group reviews by gmap_id and calculate the average rating
average_ratings = review_data.groupby('gmap_id')['rating'].mean().reset_index()
average_ratings.rename(columns={'rating': 'average_rating'}, inplace=True)

# Merge business data with average ratings
merged_data = pd.merge(business_data, average_ratings, on='gmap_id', how='inner')

# Sort by average rating in descending order and get the top 5
top_5_businesses = merged_data.sort_values(by='average_rating', ascending=False).head(5)

# Select the business name and their average rating
result = top_5_businesses[['name', 'average_rating']].to_json(orient='records')

print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

import pandas as pd

business_data = pd.DataFrame(locals()['var_function-call-6000310372648994279'])
review_data = pd.DataFrame(locals()['var_function-call-4022321191204377291'])

# Convert 'rating' column to numeric, handling potential errors by coercing to NaN
review_data['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(review_data['rating'], errors='coerce')

# Drop rows where rating is NaN (if any)
review_data.dropna(subset=['rating'], inplace=True)

# Group reviews by gmap_id and calculate the average rating
average_ratings = review_data.groupby('gmap_id')['rating'].mean().reset_index()
average_ratings.rename(columns={'rating': 'average_rating'}, inplace=True)

# Merge business data with average ratings
merged_data = pd.merge(business_data, average_ratings, on='gmap_id', how='inner')

# Sort by average rating in descending order and get the top 5
top_5_businesses = merged_data.sort_values(by='average_rating', ascending=False).head(5)

# Select the business name and their average rating
result = top_5_businesses[['name', 'average_rating']].to_json(orient='records')

print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)
